question: Comment conteneuriser l'application (Docker, Dockerfile best practices, orchestration) ?

principles.text: Docker pour la conteneurisation : 73.8% adoption (SO 2025, +17 pts en 1 an), runtime containerd (CNCF gradue), format OCI standard.

sources.name: SWEBOK v4 s6.1 — container technologies to standardize deployment

principles.text: Multi-stage builds + base minimale (JRE pas JDK, alpine) + USER non-root + .dockerignore + layered JARs (Spring Boot).
Docker Compose pour equipes 1-10 devs sur 1-3 serveurs. Kubernetes uniquement pour multi-node et auto-scaling (30% adoption vs Docker 74%).

sources.name: SWEBOK v4 s6.1 — orchestrators to improve scalability

principles.text: Valider localement avant chaque push CI quand Dockerfile ou docker-compose.yml est modifie : `docker build --check` (lint Dockerfile, < 1 sec) + `docker compose config` (validation YAML compose). Evite un pipeline complet de 10-20 min pour chaque erreur de syntaxe ou de configuration.

sources.name: Docker Blog — Introducing Docker Build Checks ("A good practice is to evaluate a new or updated Dockerfile before committing or sharing your changes")
Docker Docs — Build checks (docker build --check, "faster feedback typically completes in less than a second")
DORA — Capabilities: Continuous Integration ("Each commit should trigger a series of automated tests that provide feedback in a few minutes")
Docker Blog — Shift-Left Testing with Testcontainers ("local integration testing enables at least 65% faster defect identification than testing on a Shared Integration Environment")
Microsoft DevOps Docs — Shift testing left ("Shifting left ensures that most testing is completed before a change merges into the main branch")

plus: convergence : 5 sources independantes (Docker + DORA + Microsoft) arrivent a la meme conclusion
effet important : feedback < 1 sec vs 10-20 min pipeline, 65% reduction Lead Time for Changes

java-spring-boot.recommendation: Dockerfile multi-stage : eclipse-temurin:21-jdk (build) → eclipse-temurin:21-jre (runtime). Layered JARs via java -Djarmode=tools extract --layers. USER non-root. Image finale ~200MB vs ~800MB sans multi-stage.

sources.name: Spring Boot Container Images docs — layered JARs
Docker best practices docs — multi-stage, non-root

typescript-react-vite.recommendation: Dockerfile multi-stage : node:22-alpine (build avec pnpm) → nginx:alpine (serve static). COPY dist vers /usr/share/nginx/html. Config nginx personnalisee.
